title: Add options `tags-only` and` tags-raw` to the list-tags command

comments.body:
```
$ skopeo  list-tags docker://docker.io/fedora
{
    "Repository": "docker.io/library/fedora",
    "Tags": [
        "20",
        "21",
        "22",
        "23",
        "24",
        "25",
        "26-modular",
        "26",
        "27",
        "28",
        "29",
        "30",
        "31",
        "32",
        "33",
        "branched",
        "heisenbug",
        "latest",
        "modular",
        "rawhide"
    ]
}

$ skopeo  list-tags docker://docker.io/fedora --tags-only
"20"
"21"
"22"
"23"
"24"
"25"
"26-modular"
"26"
"27"
"28"
"29"
"30"
"31"
"32"
"33"
"branched"
"heisenbug"
"latest"
"modular"
"rawhide"

$ skopeo list-tags docker://docker.io/fedora --tags-only --tags-raw
20
21
22
23
24
25
26-modular
26
27
28
29
30
31
32
33
branched
heisenbug
latest
modular
rawhide
```
you need to gofmt your go file and repush.
A test or two would be a welcomed addition.
tks, I will try this tomorrow
Why wouldn't we just use jq to get this data?  Or add a --format to the command allow users to specify format, this is what Podman and Buildah do.

> Why wouldn't we just use jq to get this data?

It might not be available, I guess. OTOH with Skopeo itself being 22 MB large, at best, the trivial size of `jq` should be easily acceptable.

> Or add a --format to the command allow users to specify format, this is what Podman and Buildah do.

That would be a once-for-ever solution, OTOH it exposes more API that has to be kept stable. I didn’t quite follow the situation with `podman inspect` breakages — are there any lessons learned  that should be applied here?

The format changes in Podman were bugs in the comparison to Docker, But they were in the field and when we changed them to match Docker inspect we inadvertently broke existing users, which I don't think is an issue with skopeo.
I think these two options can be used to simply output tags before `--format` is implemented
I don't agree.  We don't want to have to support these flags going forward.  Either use a JSON command line processor like JQ or implement --format like Buildah and Podman have.
